assertions.description: The Worker constructor — the host this feature extends — is exposed.
Creating a Worker from a blob URL does not throw.
The Worker constructor's options bag ignores unknown keys gracefully — required for the forward-compatible document-policy member.
Response headers carry the Document-Policy / Require-Document-Policy values that workers now honour; Headers iteration is available.
Dedicated worker globals expose self.location, the place the worker's document-policy is keyed against.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const code='self.postMessage(typeof self.location)';const b=new Blob([code],{type:'application/javascript'});const u=URL.createObjectURL(b);const w=new Worker(u);return new Promise(r=>{w.onmessage=e=>{w.terminate();URL.revokeObjectURL(u);r(e.data==='object');};setTimeout(()=>{w.terminate();r(false);},2000);});}catch(_){return false;}})()

assertions.description: document.featurePolicy (deprecated) or document.policy may be exposed; in any case document is present (the carrier of policy in main thread).
